extra_fields.detailed_description: Define the specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) deliverables for the project. This includes defining the required Strehl ratio, wall-plug efficiency, and operational lifetime under specified conditions.

Establish clear and objective criteria for determining the success of the validation effort. This includes defining acceptable tolerances for measurements, statistical significance levels, and performance thresholds.

Identify and document all assumptions and constraints that may impact the project scope and objectives. This includes assumptions about the accuracy of simulation models, the availability of resources, and the stability of the testing environment.

Develop a plan for managing the project scope, including procedures for handling scope changes, documenting requirements, and ensuring that all stakeholders have a shared understanding of the project objectives.
Identify all task dependencies, determine the optimal sequence of tasks, and create a dependency diagram to visualize the project workflow. This includes identifying lead and lag times between tasks.

task_children.description: Estimate Task Durations and Resource Allocation

extra_fields.detailed_description: Estimate the duration of each task based on historical data, expert judgment, and industry best practices. Allocate resources to each task, considering their availability and skill sets. This includes identifying critical resources and potential bottlenecks.

Create a detailed project schedule using scheduling software, incorporating task dependencies, durations, and resource allocations. Identify the critical path and potential schedule risks. This includes setting milestones and tracking progress against the schedule.

task_children.description: Identify and Document Assumptions and Constraints

extra_fields.detailed_description: Identify all assumptions and constraints that may impact the project schedule, such as resource availability, budget limitations, and regulatory requirements. Document these assumptions and constraints in the project plan and develop mitigation strategies for potential risks.

Review the project plan with stakeholders and subject matter experts to ensure its accuracy and completeness. Refine the plan based on feedback and incorporate any necessary changes. This includes obtaining final approval of the project plan.

Determine the labor costs associated with each task, including salaries, benefits, and overhead. This involves consulting with HR, reviewing labor market rates, and considering the skill sets required for each task.

Identify all materials and equipment needed for the project and estimate their costs. This includes obtaining quotes from vendors, considering shipping and handling fees, and accounting for potential price fluctuations.

Create a comprehensive budget that includes all estimated costs, including labor, materials, equipment, travel, and contingency funds. This involves using a spreadsheet or project management software to track all expenses and ensure that the budget is realistic and achievable.

Develop a detailed project schedule that outlines all tasks, their durations, dependencies, and start and end dates. This involves using project management software to create a Gantt chart or other visual representation of the schedule.
Present the budget and schedule to stakeholders for approval. This involves addressing any questions or concerns, making revisions as needed, and obtaining formal sign-off from all relevant parties.
Identify all internal team members and departments involved in the project. Determine their roles, responsibilities, and level of influence.
Identify external entities such as funding agencies, suppliers, regulatory bodies, and potential research partners. Determine their interests and expectations.
Analyze the power, influence, and interests of each stakeholder. Prioritize stakeholders based on their impact on the project's success.
Create a communication plan outlining how to engage with each stakeholder group. Define communication frequency, channels, and key messages.
Set up communication channels such as regular meetings, email updates, and online collaboration platforms. Ensure stakeholders have access to relevant project information.

Identify all potential risks that could impact the project, including technical, financial, operational, and external risks. This involves brainstorming sessions, reviewing historical data, and consulting with experts.
Evaluate the likelihood of each identified risk occurring and the potential impact on the project's schedule, budget, and performance. Use qualitative and quantitative methods to prioritize risks.
Create specific mitigation plans for each high-priority risk, outlining actions to reduce the probability or impact of the risk. Assign responsibility for implementing each mitigation plan.

Document all identified risks, their probability and impact, and the corresponding mitigation strategies in a comprehensive risk response plan. This plan will serve as a guide for managing risks throughout the project lifecycle.

Determine the appropriate level of contingency reserves (budget and schedule) to address potential cost overruns or schedule delays resulting from unforeseen risks. This involves analyzing the potential impact of high-priority risks and allocating sufficient resources to mitigate them.

task_children.description: Procure Optical Engine and Mechanical Mount

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish detailed performance requirements for the optical engine, including power, wavelength, beam quality, and efficiency.  Document these specifications for vendor communication.
Research and identify potential vendors capable of supplying the required optical engine and mechanical mount. Evaluate vendors based on technical capabilities, experience, cost, and lead time.

task_children.description: Prepare and Issue Request for Proposal (RFP)

extra_fields.detailed_description: Develop a comprehensive RFP outlining the project requirements, technical specifications, and evaluation criteria. Issue the RFP to selected vendors and manage the proposal submission process.

Evaluate vendor proposals based on technical merit, cost, schedule, and compliance with requirements. Conduct vendor interviews and site visits as needed. Select the preferred vendor for the optical engine and mechanical mount.
Negotiate contract terms and conditions with the selected vendor, including pricing, delivery schedule, payment terms, and warranty. Award the contract and establish a formal agreement.

task_children.description: Procure Vibration and Thermal Testing Equipment

extra_fields.detailed_description: Determine the precise specifications for the vibration and thermal testing equipment, including frequency range, force limits, temperature range, and control accuracy. This will ensure the equipment meets the project's validation requirements.
Research and identify potential suppliers of vibration and thermal testing equipment. Evaluate suppliers based on their reputation, product quality, technical support, and pricing.

Evaluate the proposals from potential suppliers and select the equipment that best meets the project's technical and budgetary requirements. Consider factors such as performance, reliability, maintainability, and cost.

Negotiate contracts with the selected suppliers and place orders for the vibration and thermal testing equipment. Ensure that the contracts include clear specifications, delivery schedules, and warranty terms.
Coordinate the delivery and installation of the vibration and thermal testing equipment. Ensure that the equipment is properly installed and calibrated before use.

Clearly define the specifications and performance requirements for all metrology and instrumentation equipment needed for the project. This includes accuracy, resolution, range, and environmental operating conditions.
Research and identify potential suppliers of metrology and instrumentation equipment that meet the defined requirements. Consider factors such as reputation, product quality, lead times, and cost.
Evaluate the proposals from different suppliers based on technical specifications, cost, and delivery schedule. Select the equipment that best meets the project's needs and budget.
Negotiate contracts with the selected suppliers, ensuring favorable terms and conditions. Place orders for the required metrology and instrumentation equipment.
Monitor the delivery of the ordered equipment and conduct thorough inspections upon arrival to ensure that it meets the specified requirements and is free from damage.

Develop a detailed test plan outlining the specific tests to be performed on each component, including thermal cycling, vibration testing, and radiation exposure. Define acceptance criteria and data collection methods.
Subject components to repeated cycles of high and low temperatures to simulate the thermal stresses of space environment. Monitor component performance and record any failures or degradation.
Expose components to vibration profiles simulating launch and operational conditions. Monitor component performance and record any failures or degradation.

Analyze the data collected during thermal cycling and vibration testing to determine component failure rates, performance degradation, and compliance with acceptance criteria. Prepare a test report summarizing the results.
Expose components to radiation levels expected in the space environment. Monitor component performance and record any failures or degradation due to radiation effects.

task_children.description: Prepare for bakeout and contamination certification

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Prepare the system and vacuum chamber for bakeout and contamination certification.
Scope: Includes cleaning, inspection, and preparation of documentation.
Steps: Clean all components, inspect for contamination, prepare documentation for certification.
Deliverables: Clean system, completed documentation.

task_children.description: Perform bakeout of the optical system

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Remove volatile contaminants from the optical system.
Scope: Includes setting up the vacuum chamber, monitoring temperature and pressure, and ensuring proper ventilation.
Steps: Set up vacuum chamber, heat system to bakeout temperature, monitor pressure, cool down system.
Deliverables: Baked-out optical system.

Objective: Monitor contamination levels during the bakeout process.
Scope: Includes using residual gas analyzers (RGA) and other monitoring equipment to track contaminant levels.
Steps: Install RGA, monitor contaminant levels, analyze data.
Deliverables: Contamination monitoring data.

Objective: Obtain certification that the system meets contamination requirements.
Scope: Includes submitting documentation, undergoing inspection, and receiving certification.
Steps: Submit documentation, undergo inspection, receive certification.
Deliverables: Contamination certification document.

task_children.description: Integrate Optical Engine with Mechanical Mount
Align optical engine to mechanical mount

extra_fields.detailed_description: Precisely align the optical engine with the mechanical mount to ensure optimal beam propagation and stability. This involves using precision alignment tools and techniques to minimize misalignment errors.

task_children.description: Secure optical engine to mechanical mount

extra_fields.detailed_description: Securely fasten the optical engine to the mechanical mount to prevent movement or vibration during testing. This involves using appropriate fasteners and torque specifications to ensure a robust and stable connection.

Verify the compatibility of the optical engine and mechanical mount interfaces to ensure proper integration and functionality. This involves checking for mechanical fit, electrical connectivity, and communication protocols.

Conduct an initial optical performance check after integration to verify basic functionality and alignment. This involves measuring beam quality, power output, and pointing stability to ensure that the system is operating as expected.

task_children.description: Set Up Vibration and Thermal Testing Environment
Define Vibration and Thermal Test Requirements

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish specific requirements for vibration and thermal testing, including frequency ranges, temperature profiles, and acceptable tolerances. This involves reviewing project specifications, consulting with engineering teams, and defining test parameters.

task_children.description: Select Vibration and Thermal Testing Equipment

extra_fields.detailed_description: Identify and select appropriate vibration and thermal testing equipment based on the defined requirements. This includes researching available equipment, evaluating performance specifications, and considering factors such as cost, availability, and compatibility.

task_children.description: Prepare Test Site and Safety Protocols

extra_fields.detailed_description: Prepare the test site for vibration and thermal testing, ensuring that all safety protocols are in place. This includes setting up the testing equipment, implementing safety measures, and conducting safety training for personnel.

Calibrate the selected vibration and thermal testing equipment to ensure accurate and reliable measurements. This involves using calibration standards, following calibration procedures, and documenting the calibration results.
Define the installation process, identify potential challenges, and create a detailed plan for installing the metrology and instrumentation systems.
Ensure the installation site meets the requirements for the metrology and instrumentation systems, including power, network connectivity, and environmental controls.
Physically install the metrology and instrumentation hardware components, including sensors, controllers, and data acquisition systems.
Install and configure the software required for the metrology and instrumentation systems, and integrate it with the data acquisition system.
Test and verify the installation of the metrology and instrumentation systems to ensure they are functioning correctly and meeting performance requirements.

Establish specific calibration requirements for each piece of metrology equipment based on its intended use and accuracy requirements. This includes identifying calibration standards, frequency of calibration, and acceptable tolerances.

Coordinate with internal or external calibration labs to schedule calibration services for all metrology equipment. This includes reserving time slots, arranging for equipment transportation, and ensuring that calibration certificates are provided.

Conduct initial calibration checks on all metrology equipment upon receipt from the supplier or after maintenance. This involves comparing measurements against known standards and verifying that the equipment meets the specified accuracy requirements.

Create detailed documentation of all calibration procedures, including the steps involved, the equipment used, the calibration standards, and the acceptance criteria. This documentation will serve as a reference for future calibration activities and audits.

Establish a system for maintaining accurate and up-to-date calibration records for all metrology equipment. This includes tracking calibration dates, calibration certificates, and any maintenance or repairs performed on the equipment.
Develop a detailed design for the laser safety interlock system, including sensor placement, wiring diagrams, and control logic. This design must meet all applicable safety standards and regulations.

Purchase all necessary components for the laser safety interlock system, including sensors, relays, wiring, and control panels. Ensure that all components meet the required specifications and safety standards.
Install and wire all components of the laser safety interlock system according to the design specifications. Ensure that all connections are secure and properly grounded.

Thoroughly test and validate the laser safety interlock system to ensure that it functions correctly and meets all safety requirements. Document all test results and obtain approval from the Laser Safety Officer.

Create detailed documentation for the laser safety interlock system, including operating procedures, maintenance schedules, and troubleshooting guides. Ensure that all personnel are properly trained on the system's operation and safety protocols.

task_children.description: Prepare metrology equipment for baseline tests

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Ensure all metrology equipment is calibrated and ready for accurate baseline performance measurements.
Scope: Includes laser interferometers, power meters, and any other relevant instruments.
Steps: Power on equipment, perform calibration checks, verify alignment, and record calibration data.
Deliverables: Calibrated metrology equipment and documented calibration data.

Objective: Create a stable and controlled environment to minimize external influences on baseline measurements.
Scope: Includes temperature, humidity, and vibration control within the testing chamber.
Steps: Activate temperature control system, monitor humidity levels, engage vibration isolation system, and record environmental data.
Deliverables: Stable environmental conditions and documented environmental data.

Objective: Record the initial alignment state of the optical system before any thermal or vibration loading.
Scope: Includes beam alignment, phasing, and wavefront error measurements.
Steps: Perform initial alignment using alignment lasers, measure phasing accuracy, measure wavefront error, and record all alignment data.
Deliverables: Initial system alignment data and documented alignment procedures.

Objective: Create a detailed record of the procedures used to obtain baseline performance measurements.
Scope: Includes step-by-step instructions, equipment settings, and data acquisition parameters.
Steps: Write detailed procedures, review procedures with team members, and finalize the documentation.
Deliverables: Documented baseline measurement procedures.

Design the setup for applying controlled thermal loads to the system, including the heat injection system, heat rejection interface, and temperature sensors. Define the range of thermal loads to be applied and the duration of each test. Deliverables: Detailed design drawings and specifications for the thermal loading test setup.

Implement a closed-loop thermal control system to maintain precise temperature gradients and prevent thermal runaway. This includes selecting and integrating temperature controllers, sensors, and actuators. Deliverables: Functional thermal control system with documented performance characteristics.

Calibrate all temperature sensors used in the thermal loading tests to ensure accurate temperature measurements. This includes performing a multi-point calibration and documenting the calibration results. Deliverables: Calibrated temperature sensors with documented calibration curves.

Execute the thermal loading tests according to the test plan, applying the specified thermal loads and monitoring the system's performance. This includes recording temperature data, Strehl ratio, and wall-plug efficiency. Deliverables: Raw data from the thermal loading tests.

Analyze the data from the thermal loading tests to determine the system's performance under various thermal conditions. This includes calculating Strehl ratio, wall-plug efficiency, and thermal gradients. Deliverables: Report summarizing the results of the thermal loading tests.

Objective: Configure the vibration testing equipment for accurate and controlled vibration loading.
Scope: Includes mounting the system, connecting sensors, and verifying equipment calibration.
Steps: 1. Mount the system on the shaker table. 2. Connect accelerometers and other sensors. 3. Calibrate the vibration shaker. 4. Verify sensor connections and data acquisition system.
Deliverables: A fully configured and calibrated vibration test setup.

Objective: Determine the appropriate vibration levels and frequencies to simulate the space environment.
Scope: Includes analyzing launch and operational vibration data and defining test profiles.
Steps: 1. Review launch and operational vibration data. 2. Define vibration test profiles (frequency, amplitude, duration). 3. Obtain approval from the project manager.
Deliverables: Approved vibration test profiles.

Objective: Subject the system to the defined vibration loading profiles.
Scope: Includes running the vibration shaker and monitoring system performance.
Steps: 1. Run the vibration shaker according to the test profiles. 2. Monitor system alignment, phasing, and Strehl ratio. 3. Record accelerometer data.
Deliverables: Vibration test data.

Objective: Analyze the vibration test data to assess the system's performance under vibration.
Scope: Includes processing accelerometer data, evaluating system alignment and phasing, and assessing Strehl ratio degradation.
Steps: 1. Process accelerometer data. 2. Evaluate system alignment and phasing changes. 3. Assess Strehl ratio degradation. 4. Document findings.
Deliverables: Vibration test data analysis report.

task_children.description: Measure Strehl Ratio and Wall-Plug Efficiency
Calibrate power meters and optical sensors

extra_fields.detailed_description: Ensure accurate power and efficiency measurements by calibrating all relevant metrology equipment. This includes power meters, optical spectrum analyzers, and any other sensors used to measure optical power or wavelength.

task_children.description: Measure input power to the optical engine

extra_fields.detailed_description: Accurately measure the electrical power supplied to the optical engine. This involves using calibrated power meters and voltage probes to determine the input power with high precision.

task_children.description: Measure output optical power and spectrum

extra_fields.detailed_description: Measure the total optical power output from the coherent beam combining system. Also, characterize the spectral content of the output beam to ensure proper beam combining and minimize unwanted spectral components.

task_children.description: Calculate Strehl ratio from wavefront data

extra_fields.detailed_description: Determine the Strehl ratio by analyzing the wavefront data obtained from interferometric measurements. This involves processing the data to extract the wavefront error and calculating the Strehl ratio based on the Marechal approximation or other appropriate methods.

Calculate the wall-plug efficiency by dividing the total optical power output by the electrical power input. This calculation will provide a measure of the system's overall energy conversion efficiency.

Ensure accurate power measurements by calibrating all power meters and detectors used in the wall-plug efficiency measurement setup. This includes verifying calibration against NIST-traceable standards.
Measure the total laser output power before the beam combining optics. This measurement serves as the input power for the wall-plug efficiency calculation. Record data at multiple operating points.
Measure the power of the combined beam after the beam combining optics. This measurement represents the useful output power of the system. Record data at multiple operating points.
Measure the total electrical power consumed by the laser system, including the laser diodes, cooling system, and control electronics. Record data at multiple operating points.

task_children.description: Calculate Strehl ratio from wavefront data

extra_fields.detailed_description: Acquire wavefront data using the metrology system and calculate the Strehl ratio. This involves processing the wavefront data to remove noise and aberrations, and then calculating the Strehl ratio based on the residual wavefront error.

task_children.description: Define Launch and Operational Vibration Specs

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish the vibration levels and frequencies the system will experience during launch and in-space operation. This involves reviewing launch vehicle specifications, mission profiles, and spacecraft dynamics models.
Create an initial vibration test profile based on the defined vibration specifications. This profile will include frequency ranges, acceleration levels, and duration of the test.

task_children.description: Simulate System Response to Test Profile

extra_fields.detailed_description: Use FEA models to simulate the system's response to the preliminary vibration test profile. This will help identify potential resonances and areas of high stress.

task_children.description: Refine Test Profile Based on Simulation

extra_fields.detailed_description: Adjust the vibration test profile based on the simulation results. This may involve modifying frequency ranges, acceleration levels, or duration to avoid resonances and ensure adequate testing.

task_children.description: Validate Test Profile with Component Surveys

extra_fields.detailed_description: Conduct preliminary vibration surveys on critical components to validate the refined test profile and identify any component-specific vibration sensitivities.

task_children.description: Prepare vibration test setup and fixtures

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Ensure the system is properly mounted and secured for vibration testing.
Scope: Includes designing and fabricating custom fixtures, verifying alignment, and ensuring proper grounding.
Steps: Design fixtures, fabricate fixtures, verify alignment, secure system, check grounding.
Deliverables: Securely mounted system ready for vibration testing.

task_children.description: Calibrate vibration shaker and control system

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Ensure accurate and reliable vibration input during testing.
Scope: Includes calibrating the vibration shaker, verifying control system performance, and validating sensor accuracy.
Steps: Calibrate shaker, verify control system, validate sensors, document calibration.
Deliverables: Calibrated vibration system with documented performance.

Objective: Identify system resonances and optimize test parameters.
Scope: Includes performing a low-level vibration sweep, analyzing frequency response, and adjusting test profiles.
Steps: Perform vibration sweep, analyze response, adjust profiles, document findings.
Deliverables: Optimized vibration test profiles based on system characteristics.

task_children.description: Execute full vibration test per profiles

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Subject the system to the specified vibration environment.
Scope: Includes running the vibration test according to the developed profiles, monitoring system performance, and recording data.
Steps: Run vibration test, monitor performance, record data, document observations.
Deliverables: Vibration test data and observations of system performance.

Objective: Assess any damage or degradation after vibration testing.
Scope: Includes visual inspection, functional testing, and metrology measurements.
Steps: Visual inspection, functional testing, metrology measurements, document findings.
Deliverables: Report on system condition after vibration testing.
Record initial alignment and phasing before vibration.
Configure sensors and software for live data.
Continuously track alignment and phasing shifts.
Document changes and identify trends.
Implement real-time corrections to maintain phasing.

Objective: Organize and format vibration test data for efficient analysis.
Scope: Include accelerometer data, system alignment data, and Strehl ratio measurements.
Steps: 1. Convert data to a compatible format. 2. Synchronize data streams. 3. Remove noise and artifacts.
Deliverables: Cleaned and synchronized vibration test data.

Objective: Determine the primary vibration frequencies affecting system performance.
Scope: Analyze frequency spectra to identify resonant peaks.
Steps: 1. Perform FFT analysis. 2. Identify peak frequencies. 3. Correlate frequencies with system components.
Deliverables: List of dominant vibration modes and their frequencies.

Objective: Determine the impact of vibration on Strehl ratio and system alignment.
Scope: Analyze the relationship between vibration amplitude and optical performance metrics.
Steps: 1. Plot Strehl ratio vs. vibration amplitude. 2. Plot alignment error vs. vibration amplitude. 3. Calculate correlation coefficients.
Deliverables: Correlation coefficients and plots showing the relationship between vibration and optical performance.

task_children.description: Validate FEA model with test data

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Compare FEA model predictions with experimental vibration data.
Scope: Validate the model's accuracy in predicting structural response to vibration.
Steps: 1. Compare FEA mode shapes with experimental mode shapes. 2. Compare FEA frequencies with experimental frequencies. 3. Adjust FEA model parameters to improve agreement.
Deliverables: Validated FEA model.

Objective: Create a comprehensive report summarizing the vibration data analysis results.
Scope: Include data processing methods, analysis results, and conclusions.
Steps: 1. Write a detailed description of the data analysis methods. 2. Present the analysis results in a clear and concise manner. 3. Draw conclusions based on the analysis results.
Deliverables: Vibration data analysis report.

Determine the frequencies at which control-structure interaction (CSI) instabilities are occurring. This involves analyzing vibration test data and FEA models to pinpoint resonant frequencies and modes that are causing instability.

task_children.description: Design Notch Filters for CSI Mitigation

extra_fields.detailed_description: Design and implement notch filters in the control system to attenuate the frequencies identified as causing CSI instabilities. This requires careful selection of filter parameters to avoid degrading overall system performance.
Implement advanced control algorithms designed to actively damp or suppress CSI instabilities. This may involve adaptive control techniques or model-based control strategies.

Conduct iterative testing of the implemented mitigation strategies under various vibration conditions. Refine the notch filter parameters and control algorithms based on the test results to optimize performance and stability.

task_children.description: Measure Phasing Accuracy and Wavefront Error

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Prepare the interferometer for accurate phasing accuracy measurements.
Scope: Includes alignment, calibration, and stabilization of the interferometer.
Steps: Align the interferometer optics, calibrate the path length difference, stabilize the interferometer against vibrations and thermal drift.
Deliverables: A calibrated and stable interferometer ready for phasing measurements.

task_children.description: Measure Phasing Accuracy at Target Wavelength

extra_fields.detailed_description:
Objective: Obtain accurate phasing accuracy measurements at the target wavelength.
Scope: Includes data acquisition, signal processing, and error analysis.
Steps: Acquire interference fringes, process the data to extract the phase information, analyze the data to determine the phasing accuracy, quantify measurement uncertainty.
Deliverables: Phasing accuracy measurements with associated uncertainty.

Objective: Quantify the wavefront error of the combined beam.
Scope: Includes data acquisition, wavefront reconstruction, and error analysis.
Steps: Acquire interference fringes, reconstruct the wavefront from the data, analyze the wavefront to determine the wavefront error, quantify measurement uncertainty.
Deliverables: Wavefront error measurements with associated uncertainty.

Objective: Validate the accuracy and reliability of the phasing accuracy and wavefront error measurements.
Scope: Includes data analysis, error analysis, and comparison with simulation results.
Steps: Analyze the data for systematic errors, compare the measurements with simulation results, validate the accuracy of the measurements, document the validation process.
Deliverables: Validated phasing accuracy and wavefront error measurements.

task_children.description: Assess System Stability Under Thermal and Dynamic Loads

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish realistic thermal load profiles based on space environment simulations and mission requirements. This includes defining temperature ranges, gradients, and transient thermal events.
Establish realistic dynamic load profiles based on launch vehicle specifications and on-orbit operational conditions. This includes defining vibration frequencies, amplitudes, and shock events.

Implement a real-time monitoring system to track key performance metrics (Strehl ratio, wavefront error, phasing accuracy) during thermal and dynamic loading. This includes selecting appropriate sensors, data acquisition systems, and analysis software.

Analyze the data collected during thermal and dynamic loading to assess the system's stability and identify any potential instabilities or performance degradation. This includes performing frequency analysis, time-domain analysis, and statistical analysis.

Prepare a comprehensive report documenting the test setup, procedures, results, and conclusions of the system stability assessment. This includes presenting the data in a clear and concise manner, highlighting any potential issues, and recommending corrective actions.

Gather and prepare all necessary calibration standards and reference materials required for calibrating the metrology equipment. This includes verifying the accuracy and traceability of the standards.

Conduct a thorough initial calibration of all metrology equipment according to manufacturer specifications and established calibration procedures. Document all calibration results and any adjustments made.

Validate the accuracy of the calibration by performing reference measurements on known artifacts or standards. Compare the measured values with the certified values and assess the measurement uncertainty.

Create detailed documentation of the calibration procedures used, including step-by-step instructions, equipment settings, and environmental conditions. Record all calibration results, uncertainties, and any deviations from the standard procedures.

Identify the appropriate calibration standards and procedures for each piece of metrology equipment. This includes researching NIST-traceable standards and manufacturer-recommended calibration methods.
Schedule calibration services with accredited calibration labs or internal calibration facilities. Coordinate schedules to minimize downtime and ensure timely completion of calibration activities.
Before sending equipment for calibration, perform pre-calibration checks to verify functionality and identify any potential issues that may affect the calibration process.
Execute the calibration procedures according to established protocols. This includes connecting the equipment to calibration standards, taking measurements, and recording data.

task_children.description: Analyze Calibration Data and Generate Reports

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze the calibration data to determine the accuracy and uncertainty of the metrology equipment. Generate calibration reports documenting the calibration results and any corrective actions taken.
Establish quantifiable metrics (e.g., Strehl ratio, wavefront error) to evaluate the effectiveness of alignment procedures. Define acceptable ranges for each metric.
Create a script to automate the alignment process, incorporating feedback from metrology data. The script should be able to adjust optical elements to optimize alignment metrics.
Evaluate the performance of the alignment procedures under thermal and dynamic loading conditions. Identify any limitations or instabilities.

task_children.description: Refine Alignment Procedures Based on Results

extra_fields.detailed_description: Iteratively improve the alignment procedures based on the test results. Address any identified limitations or instabilities. Document the final alignment procedures.
Identify key parameters for thermal, structural, and optical behavior. Determine parameter ranges and sensitivities.
Create a detailed FEA model of the optical engine and mechanical mount. Incorporate thermal and structural properties.
Create an optical simulation model using Zemax or similar software. Model wavefront propagation and Strehl ratio.
Couple the FEA and optical models to simulate TSO effects. Develop a scaling methodology for larger apertures.
Calibrate individual thermal, structural and optical models against existing data. Validate sub-models before integration.

task_children.description: Validate TSO Model with Experimental Data
Define Validation Metrics and Acceptance Criteria

extra_fields.detailed_description: Establish specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound (SMART) metrics for validating the TSO model against experimental data. Define clear acceptance criteria for determining whether the model is sufficiently accurate.
Develop a detailed experimental plan for validating the TSO model. This includes selecting appropriate test conditions, defining measurement procedures, and identifying necessary instrumentation.
Conduct the validation experiments according to the experimental plan. This includes setting up the experiment, collecting data, and monitoring the system's performance.

task_children.description: Compare Model Predictions with Experimental Data

extra_fields.detailed_description: Compare the TSO model predictions with the experimental data. This includes analyzing the data, calculating the validation metrics, and assessing whether the acceptance criteria are met.

Prepare a detailed report documenting the validation experiments, the data analysis, and the comparison between the model predictions and the experimental data. Include a discussion of the strengths and limitations of the TSO model.

Develop a detailed plan outlining the types of boundary conditions to measure (thermal, mechanical), the sensors to use, and the measurement locations. This plan should also include calibration procedures and data acquisition methods.

Identify and procure appropriate sensors for measuring thermal and mechanical boundary conditions. Consider factors such as accuracy, resolution, range, and environmental compatibility. Ensure sensors are calibrated and traceable to national standards.

task_children.description: Design and Fabricate Sensor Mounting Fixtures

extra_fields.detailed_description: Design and fabricate custom mounting fixtures to securely attach sensors to the test article and ensure accurate measurements. Consider factors such as thermal conductivity, mechanical stability, and ease of installation and removal.

Install sensors and mounting fixtures on the test article. Perform measurements of thermal and mechanical boundary conditions under various operating conditions. Record data using a data acquisition system.

Analyze the measured boundary condition data to ensure accuracy and consistency. Compare the data with theoretical predictions and historical data. Identify and resolve any discrepancies or anomalies.

task_children.description: Predict Performance for Larger Aperture Sizes

extra_fields.detailed_description: Specify the exact aperture size (e.g., diameter, number of tiles) and configuration (e.g., hexagonal, circular) for the larger aperture system to be simulated. This includes defining the spacing between tiles and any obscurations within the aperture.

Create a detailed simulation model of the larger aperture system using appropriate software tools (e.g., Zemax, Code V, ANSYS). This model should incorporate all relevant optical, thermal, and structural effects, including wavefront aberrations, thermal distortions, and mechanical stresses.

Run simulations of the larger aperture system under a range of operating conditions, including different thermal loads, vibration levels, and atmospheric turbulence profiles. Collect data on key performance metrics such as Strehl ratio, beam quality, and power throughput.

task_children.description: Analyze Simulation Results and Identify Trends

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze the simulation data to identify trends and relationships between operating conditions and system performance. Determine the sensitivity of the system to different parameters and identify potential bottlenecks or limitations.

Prepare a detailed report documenting the simulation setup, including the model parameters, operating conditions, and simulation results. This report should include figures, tables, and graphs to illustrate the key findings and conclusions.

task_children.description: Refine Scaling Model Based on Validation Results
Compare model predictions to validation results

extra_fields.detailed_description: Compare the scaling model predictions with the experimental validation results to identify discrepancies and areas for improvement. This involves analyzing the differences between the predicted and measured Strehl ratio and wall-plug efficiency for various aperture sizes and operating conditions.

task_children.description: Identify sources of discrepancies in model

extra_fields.detailed_description: Analyze the discrepancies between the scaling model predictions and experimental validation results to identify the root causes of the differences. This may involve examining the model assumptions, material properties, boundary conditions, and numerical methods to pinpoint the sources of error.

task_children.description: Adjust model parameters based on analysis

extra_fields.detailed_description: Adjust the parameters of the scaling model based on the analysis of the discrepancies between the model predictions and experimental validation results. This may involve modifying the material properties, boundary conditions, or numerical methods to improve the accuracy of the model.

Re-validate the refined scaling model with the experimental validation results to ensure that the adjustments have improved the accuracy of the model. This involves comparing the predictions of the refined model with the experimental data and assessing the level of agreement.

task_children.description: Document model refinement process and results

extra_fields.detailed_description: Document the entire model refinement process, including the analysis of the discrepancies, the adjustments made to the model parameters, and the results of the re-validation. This documentation should provide a clear and concise record of the model refinement process and the improvements achieved.

task_children.description: Gather Raw Data for Progress Reports

extra_fields.detailed_description: Collect all relevant data points from ongoing experiments, simulations, and analyses. This includes Strehl ratio measurements, wall-plug efficiency data, vibration test results, metrology data, and scaling model predictions. Ensure data is properly formatted and stored in a central repository.

task_children.description: Analyze Data and Identify Key Trends

extra_fields.detailed_description: Perform statistical analysis on the collected data to identify key trends, anomalies, and deviations from expected performance. Calculate relevant metrics, such as mean, standard deviation, and correlation coefficients. Visualize data using charts and graphs to facilitate interpretation.

task_children.description: Document Findings and Prepare Report Draft

extra_fields.detailed_description: Summarize the key findings from the data analysis in a clear and concise manner. Prepare a draft progress report that includes a description of the experimental setup, data collection methods, analysis techniques, and key results. Include relevant charts, graphs, and tables to support the findings.

Circulate the draft progress report to relevant stakeholders for review and feedback. Incorporate feedback from stakeholders to improve the clarity, accuracy, and completeness of the report. Address any questions or concerns raised by stakeholders.

Finalize the progress report based on the feedback received from stakeholders. Ensure that the report is properly formatted and meets all requirements. Submit the report to the funding agency and other relevant stakeholders.
Create a standardized template for documenting test procedures and results, ensuring consistency and completeness.
Thoroughly document the test setup, including equipment used, connections, and environmental conditions.
Detail each step of the test procedure, including parameters, settings, and expected outcomes.
Record all test results, including measurements, observations, and any anomalies encountered.
Review the completed documentation for accuracy, completeness, and clarity, ensuring it meets project standards.

Define the sections, subsections, and key data points to be included in the final validation report. This includes determining the level of detail required for each section and ensuring alignment with stakeholder expectations.

Gather all relevant data from performance target validation, vibration qualification, metrology accuracy validation, and scaling model validation. Analyze the data to identify trends, anomalies, and key findings.
Write the individual sections of the final validation report, including introductions, methodology descriptions, results presentations, and conclusions. Prepare executive summaries for each section.
Conduct a thorough review of the draft report to ensure accuracy, clarity, and completeness. Incorporate feedback from stakeholders and make necessary revisions.
Format the report according to established guidelines, obtain final approvals, and publish the report in a designated repository. Distribute the report to relevant stakeholders.

Develop a comprehensive slide deck summarizing the project's goals, methodology, key findings, and conclusions. Include visual aids such as charts, graphs, and images to effectively communicate complex information.
Coordinate with stakeholders to find a mutually convenient time for the presentation. Send out meeting invitations with a detailed agenda and relevant background information.
Conduct a practice run of the presentation to ensure smooth delivery and effective time management. Solicit feedback from colleagues to identify areas for improvement.
Present the project findings to stakeholders, addressing any questions or concerns they may have. Ensure that all key messages are clearly communicated and understood.
Document any feedback received from stakeholders during the presentation. Develop a plan to address any concerns or suggestions raised, and communicate the plan to stakeholders.

Objective: Structure project data for easy retrieval. Scope: Organize all documents, test results, and reports. Steps: Create a folder structure, label files, and remove duplicates. Deliverables: Organized project data.

task_children.description: Backup Project Data to Secure Storage

extra_fields.detailed_description: Objective: Ensure data preservation. Scope: Copy all project data to a secure storage location. Steps: Select storage medium, initiate backup, and verify completion. Deliverables: Backed-up project data.
Objective: Confirm data accuracy after backup. Scope: Check data integrity in the archive. Steps: Run checksums, compare files, and validate data. Deliverables: Verified data integrity.

Objective: Record archiving details. Scope: Document the archiving process and storage location. Steps: Write a procedure, note storage details, and store the document. Deliverables: Archiving documentation.
